mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market slack reduces wage inflation pressures and signals economic weakness, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of periods where UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, which drives corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to predict positive 60-day returns with an out-of-sample accuracy exceeding 52 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals tightening supply capacity and rising input costs, which compress profit margins and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week during late-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields positive 5-day returns in late-cycle regimes with a t-statistic greater than 1.96.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rates above 4.0 percent impose severe financing constraints on highly leveraged firms, reducing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in 60 percent of observations when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total employment combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports consumer spending and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal of PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline shows a rank information coefficient below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in housing starts reflect rising household confidence and construction sector activity, which propagate through supply chains to boost corporate revenues and yield positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: HOUST increases fail to predict positive 60-day returns with statistical significance at the 95 percent confidence level.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production below 110 indicates severe supply-side contraction, which triggers aggressive monetary easing expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week during post-shock recovery phases.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50 percent of periods where INDPRO falls below 110 during recovery regimes.
